_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Ability to distinguish between problems that require machine learning and problems that can be solved more simply and reliably with rule-based logic or statistical methods.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-A-002 (AB, cosine: 0.538), AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.516).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.419). Shared vocabulary: [ability]. DCWF-only terms [analysis, dynamic, perform] vs WIDAI-only terms [based, distinguish, learning, logic, machine, methods] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.888 (P=0.925, R=0.855). P exceeds R by 0.071 — the DCWF element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "Ability to perform dynamic analysis." and "Ability to distinguish between problems that require machine learning and proble" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.542 > STS 0.419 (gap +0.123). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ability] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.7%. Shared tokens: [ability]. DCWF-only: [analysis, dynamic, perform]. WIDAI-only: [based, distinguish, learning, logic, machine, methods].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.123 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
